source.title: Accident Investigation Report - MP 270 Platform B Glycol Burn Incident

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Main Pass Block 270

actions_taken: IP evacuated via helicopter to onshore heliport at 1645 hours
IP transported to Plaquemines Medical Center in Port Sulphur, LA
Burns scrubbed and cleaned at medical facility
IP released to Restricted Work duty for three days
IP placed in office doing administrative work
WOG issued Operational Safety Alert OSA-2023-014 on 19 July 2023
event.summary: On 3 July 2023 at approximately 1545 hours, an 'A' Operator employed by Island Operating Company was testing the Level Safety High/Low (LSHL) on the Glycol Reboiler at Main Pass 270 Platform B when a blockage in the bridle occurred. As the IP cleared the obstruction, heated Triethylene Glycol (350°F) drained out of the bridle and splashed onto the IP, resulting in severe 2nd degree burns to approximately 27% of the body (abdomen, arms, and side). The IP was wearing PPE adequate for ambient temperature liquids but not suitable for 350°F glycol. The IP followed proper testing procedures, but the JSA overlooked the thermal hazard of hot glycol splash.
recommendations: Ensure proper PPE is worn in accordance with MSDS for Triethylene Glycol and protection from thermal risk of hot liquids
Improve JSA to identify thermal hazards and potential for blocked or plugged equipment
Raise awareness of hazards personnel could be exposed to when working with hot liquids and chemicals
key_phrases: manage potential blocked or plugged equipment

threats.name: Blockage in LSHL bridle on Glycol Reboiler

consequences.name: Severe 2nd degree burns to personnel (27% body surface)

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for LSHL testing
controls.lod_basis: Planning and hazard identification before work

supporting_text: The JSA acknowledged the presence of a hazardous substance but missed the section of the JSA form 'Other Hazards' and the possible obstacle of having to 'manage potential blocked or plugged equipment'
the possibility of being splashed by heated Glycol was overlooked

controls.name: Testing procedures for LSHL
controls.lod_basis: Operational procedures to control work execution

supporting_text: Further investigation determined that the IP did follow the proper testing procedures provided by IOC
However, the possibility of being splashed by heated Glycol was overlooked

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) for chemical and thermal hazards
controls.barrier_role: Personal protection from hot liquid and chemical exposure
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to protect worker from hazard contact

supporting_text: The IP was wearing Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) at the time of the incident, and though it was adequate for working with ambient temperature liquids, it was not suitable for working with the 350°F Glycol
It was also determined that the PPE worn by the IP was inadequate and should have provided protection for chemical risk and hot liquid (thermal) risk
Supervision – Not ensuring the use of proper PPE: IP should have had on PPE that was in accordance with the MSDS for Triethylene Glycol as well as protection from the thermal risk of the hot Glycol

controls.name: MSDS for Triethylene Glycol
controls.barrier_role: Hazard information and safe handling guidance
controls.lod_basis: Information resource for hazard awareness and control selection

supporting_text: The AI also received the 'Operational Safety Alert' put out by WOG on 19 July 2023
Those documents include the Job Safety Analysis (JSA), Testing procedures, and Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) for Triethylene Glycol
IP should have had on PPE that was in accordance with the MSDS for Triethylene Glycol

controls.name: Supervision of work activities
controls.barrier_role: Oversight to ensure proper hazard controls and PPE selection
controls.lod_basis: Verification and oversight of frontline work execution

supporting_text: Supervision – Not ensuring the use of proper PPE: IP should have had on PPE that was in accordance with the MSDS for Triethylene Glycol as well as protection from the thermal risk of the hot Glycol

controls.name: Emergency medical response and evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Emergency response to minimize injury severity

supporting_text: At approximately 1645 hours, the IP departed the platform on the loop helicopter and was flown to the onshore heliport
From there the IP drove his personal vehicle, accompanied by another crew member, to an onshore medical facility for evaluation
The IP was treated and released to RW duty

controls.name: Level Safety High/Low (LSHL) system on Glycol Reboiler
controls.barrier_role: Process control to prevent overfill/underfill conditions

supporting_text: an 'A' Operator (IP) employed by Island Operating Company (IOC) was performing monthly testing of the LSHL located on the Glycol Reboiler (EBC 2151)
when the IP encountered a blockage within the bridle

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
